steps.title: Find the antecedent
steps.content: The previous sentence names two woodcuts: Evening at Carl Inn and Lake Basin in the High Sierra. The blank refers back to both works, so it needs a plural pronoun with a verb that fits the 2019 exhibition context.

steps.title: Check agreement
steps.content: A and C use singular pronouns, so they do not agree with the two woodcuts. D is plural, but some is vague because the sentence is pointing to the two named works specifically. The remaining choice clearly refers to both woodcuts. The answer is B.
